steps.title: Identify the legs
steps.content:
The longest side is the hypotenuse.
$$\sqrt{82}$$
The legs are
$$4\sqrt{2} \quad \text{and} \quad 5\sqrt{2}$$
because
$$(4\sqrt{2})^2+(5\sqrt{2})^2=32+50=82$$

steps.title: Use right triangle area
steps.content:
The area of a right triangle is one half the product of its legs.
$$A=\frac{1}{2}(4\sqrt{2})(5\sqrt{2})$$

steps.title: Simplify the product
steps.content:
Multiply the coefficients and radicals.
$$A=\frac{1}{2}(20)(2)$$
$$A=20$$
The answer is B.
